subject: DevOps
title: The Agile Roots
description: A quick win: understand The Agile Roots.

hook.text: Agile taught teams to ship software in weeks, not years. But Ops still deployed quarterly.

buildup.text: Developers iterated fast. Operations couldn't keep up. A bottleneck formed at deployment.

discovery.text: Agile stopped at the code handoff. Nobody extended its principles to infrastructure and releases.

twist.text: Patrick Debois saw this gap in 2008. He organized the first DevOpsDays conference in Ghent, Belgium.

climax.text: DevOps extended Agile beyond code. Build, test, deploy, and monitor — all one continuous flow.

punchline.text: Agile started it. DevOps finished the loop.

quiz.question: Who organized the first DevOpsDays conference?
